items.text: Hunk resolution tries entity-guided placement first (tier 1), then context-anchored search (tier 2), then header correction + diffy (tier 3)
Tier 1 reads the @@ semantic anchor text (after the second @@) and uses it to locate the target entity
Tier 1 scopes context search to the matched entity's byte range when entity is found
Tier 2 full-file context-anchored search runs exact match, then whitespace-insensitive, then trim-insensitive
Tier 3 header correction + diffy is the last resort, not the first attempt
Resolution tier used is reported per-hunk in the tool result
norn crate has an optional dependency on libyggd behind a cargo feature flag named libyggd-ast
EntityExtractor trait defined with fn extract(&self, source: &str, path: &Path) -> Option<Vec<Entity>>
ApplyPatchTool holds Option<Arc<dyn EntityExtractor>> — None when libyggd-ast feature disabled, Some when enabled
libyggd-ast feature provides a concrete EntityExtractor implementation that calls libyggd::ast::extract_entities()
norn-cli crate enables the libyggd-ast feature
When libyggd-ast is disabled, tier 1 is skipped and resolution starts at tier 2 (context-anchored search)
Existing SupportedLanguage enum and parallel tree-sitter code in ast.rs replaced by EntityExtractor trait
Entity extraction supports 18+ languages via libyggd (Rust, TypeScript, Python, Go, Java, C, C++, and more)
Patches with --- /dev/null as source create new files
Pre-validate skips file-exists and read-before-edit checks for /dev/null source blocks
Execute starts from an empty string for /dev/null source blocks instead of reading the file
Parent directories are created if needed for new files (same behavior as Write tool)
AST validation runs on new file content before writing
/dev/null source detected during block parsing, not as a special case in pre-validate
Patches with +++ /dev/null as target delete the target file
Pre-validate rejects patches where a later hunk modifies a file that an earlier hunk deletes
Execute captures full file content as before-content before deleting
Convention checks do not run on deleted files
Deletion recorded in action log mutation ledger as status deleted with before-content
dry_run boolean parameter added to apply_patch input schema
Dry-run resolves all hunks and validates staged content without writing to disk
Dry-run output includes per-file resolution details: tier used, entity matched, line drift
Dry-run output includes AST validation result (pass/fail with diagnostics)
Dry-run output includes blast radius: lines added, removed, modified per file and containing symbols
Dry-run output includes file length impact (before/after line counts)
Dry-run output includes diagnostics preview matching what DiagnosticsPostCheck would report
Dry-run produces identical resolution to actual application — same code path, only disk-write skipped
Dry-run results stored in the action log
mode parameter added to apply_patch input schema with values auto, strict, structural
auto mode (default) uses entity-first resolution with full tier fallback
strict mode only applies when context lines match exactly at the stated line position
strict mode reports what structural matching would have found when exact matching fails
strict mode structural_alternative includes would_apply, entity, entity_range, matched_at_line, drift, confidence
structural mode skips diffy entirely and requires entity resolution for every hunk
Failed strict-mode result includes a follow-up action ID for applying with structural matching
Patches can be assigned an artifact ID via an artifact_id parameter
Artifact storage uses the existing session event stream
Artifacts persist across turns but not across sessions
inspect_patch follow-up allows viewing a stored artifact by ID, optionally filtered by file or hunk index
edit_patch follow-up allows amending a specific hunk in a stored artifact before applying
apply_patch accepts artifact_id to apply a previously stored artifact
Artifact summary includes file count, hunk count, and blast radius estimate
Tool result always reports committed state accurately even when post-validation fails (depends NTI-001)
Model receives both the error reason and the committed output (files_modified, diagnostics, committed flag) when Gate-mode post-validation fails
Patch input format remains standard unified diff — no proprietary format required from models (CO1)
Entity resolution does not add perceptible latency to simple patches — libyggd entity extraction is lazy, invoked only when @@ anchor contains a name (CO2)
Dry-run produces identical resolution to actual application — same code path, disk-write step skipped (CO3)
File creation via /dev/null creates parent directories same as Write tool (CO5)
Tool remains usable without libyggd — libyggd-ast feature flag is optional, entity-first tier skipped when disabled (CO6)
